tavily.raw: {"query":"TSMC Q2 2026 net profit revenue guidance capex raised full-year forecast Reuters July 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://x.com/Finsee_main/status/2077704581392248896","title":"3Q26 guidance of $44.6-45.8B implies 37% YoY growth","content":"$TSM Q2 2026 earnings: Guidance Jumps to 40% Growth as AI Demand Overwhelms Supply TSMC delivered revenue of $40.2 billion, at the very top of its guidance range, up 33.7% YoY. Operating margin of 60.3% blew past the high end of guidance (58.5%), and net income surged 77% YoY to NT$706.6 billion. One caveat on earnings quality: NT$63.2 billion of that came from a one-time gain on the sale of Vanguard International Semiconductor shares - roughly 9% of net income. The bigger news is the outlook: full-year 2026 revenue growth was raised from 'above 30%' to 'slightly above 40%' in USD terms, the capital budget jumped from $52-56 billion to $60-64 billion, and TSMC announced an additional $100 billion investment in Arizona. Best 0% APR cards. 2. Best high-yield savings accounts. 3. Best money market accounts. # TSMC Raised Its 2026 Revenue Guidance: What It Means for AI Chip Demand. (TSMC) raised its full-year 2026 revenue growth guidance to slightly above 40%, up from more than 30%, after the second-quarter profit hit a record, and artificial intelligence (AI) chip demand held firm.**. The chipmaker raised its 2026 capital spending target to between $60 billion and $64 billion. It also pledged a further $100 billion for chip factories in Arizona. The result beat the NT$632.6 billion analyst forecast and marked a record high. TSMC now expects 2026 capital spending of $60 billion to $64 billion. For the third quarter, TSMC guided revenue of $44.6 billion to $45.8 billion. ## What TSMC's 2026 Revenue Guidance Signals for Chip Demand. Read the Original story TSMC Raised Its 2026 Revenue Guidance: What It Means for AI Chip Demand by Kamina Bashir at beincrypto.com.","score":0.8997663,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://investor.tsmc.com/english/encrypt/files/encrypt_file/reports/2026-07/a80d7933be643644081584087731f73b22ea5a2c/2Q26%20EarningsRelease.pdf","title":"TSMC Reports Second Quarter EPS of NT$27.25","content":"July 16, 2026 TSMC Page 1 of 2 Hsinchu, Taiwan, R.O.C., July 16, 2026 -- TSMC today announced consolidated revenue of NT$1,270.38 billion, net income of NT$706.56 billion, and diluted earnings per share of NT$27.25 (US$4.31 per ADR unit) for the second quarter ended June 30, 2026. In US dollars, second quarter revenue was $40.20 billion, which increased 33.7% year-over-year and increased 12.0% from the previous quarter. “Moving into third quarter 2026, we expect our business to be supported by continued strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies, including the steep ramp-up of our 2-nanometer technology.” Based on the Company’s current business outlook, management expects the overall performance for third quarter 2026 to be as follows: • Revenue is expected to be between US$44.6 billion and US$45.8 billion; And, based on the exchange rate assumption of 1 US dollar to 32 NT dollars, • Gross profit margin is expected to be between 65% and 67%; • Operating profit margin is expected to be between 56% and 58%.","score":0.8815438,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/Reuters/posts/tsmc-raised-its-annual-revenue-forecast-and-said-it-was-stepping-up-capital-spen/1521131963210864","title":"TSMC raised its annual revenue forecast and said it was ...","content":"TSMC also expects record CapEx in 2026, at $52-56 billion, up +32% YoY, to expand global manufacturing capacity. 56 billion dollars.","score":0.8679453,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/tsmc-seen-riding-ai-boom-fifth-straight-quarter-record-profit-2026-07-14","title":"TSMC seen riding AI boom to fifth straight quarter of record ...","content":"[Skip to main content](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/tsmc-seen-riding-ai-boom-fifth-straight-quarter-record-profit-2026-07-14/#main-content). *   [World](https://www.reuters.com/world/). ## [Browse World](https://www.reuters.com/world/). *   [Business](https://www.reuters.com/business/). ## [Browse Business](https://www.reuters.com/business/). *   [Take Five](https://www.reuters.com/business/take-five/). *   [Markets](https://www.reuters.com/markets/). ## [Browse Markets](https://www.reuters.com/markets/).
